title: Limber Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Limber Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: Limber Wildlife Sanctuary or Kazing Wildlife Sanctuary (also written as Qazing) is situated in Limber in Baramulla district in Indian union territory of Jammu & Kashmir.
overview.description: Limber Wildlife Sanctuary or Kazing Wildlife Sanctuary (also written as Qazing) is situated in Limber in Baramulla district in Indian union territory of Jammu & Kashmir. It is located, near Baramulla city and Srinagar. It is the fourth national park in the state which focuses the attention towards conserving the rare markhor wild goat. It is also a part of an eco-sensitive zone as notified by the Government of Jammu & Kashmir.

topPlaces.name: Uri Dam
topPlaces.description: Uri Dam, refers to the existing Uri-I Stage-I Hydroelectric Dam Project with 480 MW hydroelectric power generation capacity and the downstream under-construction Uri-I Stage-II Hydroelectric Dam Project with 240 MW hydroelectric power generation capacity, on the Jhelum River near Uri in Baramula district of the Jammu and Kashmir in India. Operated by the NHPC and located very near to the Line of Control - the de facto border between India and Pakistan, both are run-of-the-river projects because the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) gives Pakistan the exclusive right to regulate the Jhelum River.

topPlaces.name: Boniyar
topPlaces.description: Boniyar is a town and a municipal committee in Boniyar tehsil, Baramulla district in India's northernmost union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It is located 20 km towards west from district headquarters Baramulla.

topPlaces.name: Kathal Hil Patribal
topPlaces.description: Kathal Hil Patribal is a village in Boniyar Tehsil in the Baramulla district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is located close to the town of Uri.

Operated by the NHPC and located very near to the Line of Control - the de facto border between India and Pakistan, both are run-of-the-river projects because the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) gives Pakistan the exclusive right to regulate the Jhelum River.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Limber Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Limber Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Limber Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (60 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (152 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Limber Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Limber Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Limber Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Limber Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Limber Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Uri Dam, Boniyar, Kathal Hil Patribal.
